name: County Fair Banana Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
For the crust:
1. Chop pecans very fine, preferably in a food processor fitted with a metal blade.
2. Mix pecans and other crust ingredients in a bowl until well blended.
3. Divide into two portions and press into the bottom of 2 pie pans.
4. Chill for 30 minutes.
5. Bake at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 25 minutes. Cool completely.
1. Melt chocolate with butter and corn syrup in the top of a double boiler set over hot, not boiling water. Stir frequently.
2. Remove from heat and cool, then stir in vanilla.
3. Divide between cooled pie crusts and spread evenly.
For the filling:
1. Heat milk in a medium saucepan over medium heat until it begins to scald.
2. Beat egg yolks and gradually add sugar.
3. Gradually stir in flour.
4. Gradually stir in hot milk into the egg yolk mixture in the saucepan. Cook, stirring constantly, over medium-high heat (use a whisk). The mixture will lump as it thickens.
5. Bring to a boil and boil for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
6. Remove from the pan and continue to stir until smooth.
7. Beat in butter and optional rum.
8. Cool to room temperature.
9. Peel, slice very thin, and toss with lemon juice.
10. Whip cream until firm but not stiff.
11. Mix about a quarter of the cream into the cooled egg mixture to lighten it.
12. Fold the remaining cream and bananas into the egg mixture.
13. Fill pastry shells with banana cream and smooth to even.
14. Beat remaining cream until stiff.
15. Spoon around the edge of the pies and sprinkle with chopped pecans, if desired. Makes 2 pies.
